reply_text: ​@peterstorictrue, but as a long time anime fan, I really don't understand the obsession with live action. I don't think any of Disney's remakes were better than the original cartoon versions, and shows like Dragon Ball were a disaster. Ghost in Shell was decent because at least there was a new story. These big large American corporates come along almost saying 'cartoons aren't a respectable format but we will legitimise it by turning it into live action'. It's almost an insult to the creators and the anime/manga industry. One Piece has been around since 1997, and nobody tried to help grow and support the show. It's only after all this time that companies like Netflix and lego are cashing in with the same old maximum return for minimal effort simply re-hashing stories. What value does this series add to the franchise? As far as I can see, nothing except telling a story to people who don't respect anime as a legitimate medium.
